info.title: ThreatModeler API Documentation
info.description:
This document describes all available endpoints of ThreatModeler Platform.

Every API access requires Authorization. For Authorization you can either use ThreatModeler generated API Key or initate Login process by clicking Authorize button.

get.summary: Refreshes the list of VPCs for a given AWS third-party account from the AWS Accelerator screen.

parameters.name: thirdpartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the AWS third-party account.

post.summary: Synchronizes and saves VPCs for a given AWS third-party account.
post.description:
This method fetches VPC components from AWS using the provided credentials and regions,
saves the VPCs to the third-party mapping repository, and refreshes associated security groups.
Handles different AWS account types and credential scenarios.

parameters.name: ThirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third-party AWS account.

parameters.name: isSyncMode
parameters.description:
If `true`, performs synchronization only without creating a new third-party info record.
If `false`, creates a new third-party info record if components are found.

requestBody.description: The third-party AWS account information model.

get.summary: Gets all AWS tag keys for the specified third party account.
get.description:
This API endpoint retrieves a list of tag keys available in the AWS account associated with the provided thirdPartyInfoId.
It is useful for filtering and categorizing AWS resources by their tags within the ThreatModeler platform.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the AWS third party account.

get.summary: Gets all AWS tag values for the specified tag key and third party account.
get.description:
This API endpoint retrieves a list of tag values for a given tag key in the AWS account associated with the provided thirdPartyInfoId.
It is useful for filtering and categorizing AWS resources by their tag values within the ThreatModeler platform.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the AWS third party account.

parameters.name: tagkey
parameters.description: The tag key for which to retrieve values.

options.summary: Gets the count of AWS resources for the specified third party account and tag filters.
options.description:
This API endpoint retrieves the total number of AWS resources in the account associated with the provided thirdPartyInfoId,
optionally filtered by the specified tag filters. It is useful for understanding resource distribution and applying tag-based queries
within the ThreatModeler platform.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the AWS third party account.

requestBody.description: A list of tag filters to apply when counting resources.

get.summary: Synchronizes the Threat Model with its mapped AWS VPC from the Diagram screen.
get.description:
When a Threat Model is out of sync, a Drift icon appears, allowing manual synchronization with AWS.
This option is available only for Threat Models created from a VPC.

parameters.name: vpcProjectMappingId
parameters.description: The ID of the VPC project mapping.

parameters.name: vpcId

parameters.name: vpcProjectMappingId

get.description:
From the Diagram screen, select the VPC info option to view the sync history for that Threat Model.
This option is available only when the Threat Model is created from a VPC.

parameters.name: vpcProjectMappingId

get.summary: Enables or disables automatic threat model update in the next AWS sync for a specific VPC mapping.

parameters.name: awsSchedulerHistoryId
parameters.description: The AWS scheduler history record ID.

parameters.name: autoUpdateThreatModelInNextSync
parameters.description: If `true`, the threat model will be auto-updated in the next sync; otherwise, it will not.

parameters.name: vpcProjectMappingId
parameters.description: Optional. The VPC project mapping ID to apply the auto-update setting to.

get.summary: Checks if a scheduler job is currently running for the specified VPC project mapping.
get.description: This endpoint is used to verify the running status of a scheduler job associated with a VPC project mapping.

parameters.name: jobKeyName
parameters.description: The key name of the scheduler job to check.

get.summary: Gets the list of currently running scheduler jobs for VPCs in AWS Accelerator.
get.description: This endpoint returns all active scheduler jobs related to VPC operations in AWS Accelerator.

post.summary: Removes a running scheduler job for a specified VPC in AWS Accelerator.
post.description:
This endpoint allows you to remove a running scheduler job associated with a VPC operation in AWS Accelerator.
It is typically used to stop or clean up jobs that are no longer needed or are stuck.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key or name of the scheduler job to remove.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value or type associated with the scheduler job (e.g., "AWS", "AZUREPORTAL").

get.summary: Returns all the components that exist only in AWS or Azure, meaning those components do not exist in your Threat Model.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The Threat Model ID.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of AWS Organizations from AWS for the specified third-party account.
get.description:
This endpoint synchronizes AWS Organizations units and accounts from AWS.
If the master account matches the current instance, organizations are retrieved directly.
Otherwise, temporary credentials are used to access organizations for the specified account or role.
The organizations are then saved to the local repository.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third-party AWS account. If `0`, uses the master account or role ARN for retrieval.

get.summary: Retrieves all AWS resources for a specified third-party account and region.
get.description: This endpoint fetches all AWS resources associated with the provided third-party account and region.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third-party AWS account.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: The AWS region to retrieve resources from. Defaults to "us-east-1".

post.summary: Sets up a new company account via AWS Marketplace.
post.description:
This endpoint creates a new department, user, and library for the company.
It also assigns roles and licenses to the user.

requestBody.description: The account setup details including company name, user info, and license count.

post.summary: Setup account with AWS Marketplace to purchase license

requestBody.description: Company and license information

get.summary: Retrieves all Azure resources for the specified third party information ID.
get.description:
This API calls Azure to get all resources and their data flow relations, then saves the resources in the database.
- Requires CloudModelerEditionFeature license.
- Returns a list of resources retrieved from Azure.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third party information used to set Azure credentials.

get.summary: Retrieves all Azure virtual networks for the specified third party information ID.
get.description:
This API calls Azure to get all virtual networks associated with the provided credentials.
- Requires CloudModelerEditionFeature license.
- Returns a list of virtual networks retrieved from Azure.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third party information used to set Azure credentials.

get.summary: Retrieves all Azure subscriptions for the specified third party information ID.
get.description:
This API calls Azure to get all subscriptions associated with the provided credentials.
- Requires CloudModelerEditionFeature license.
- Returns a list of subscriptions retrieved from Azure.

parameters.name: thirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third party information used to set Azure credentials.

get.summary: Refreshes the list of Azure virtual networks (VNets) for the specified third party information ID.
get.description:
This API synchronizes and saves the latest VNets from Azure for the provided credentials.
- Requires CloudModelerEditionFeature license.
- Updates the VNet list in the database.
- Returns operation status and error details if applicable.

parameters.name: thirdpartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third party information used to set Azure credentials.

post.summary: Creates a new Threat Model from the specified Azure VNet or Resource Group mappings.
post.description:
This API performs the following actions for each VNet or Resource Group mapping:
<list type="number"><item>Saves or updates the VPCProjectMapping record.</item><item>Sets Azure credentials using the provided third party information.</item><item>Retrieves all Azure resources and their data flow relations.</item><item>Generates a diagram for the resources.</item><item>Prepares and imports the diagram into a new Threat Model project.</item><item>Associates the created project with the VPC mapping.</item><item>Validates and updates Security Requirement statuses.</item><item>Updates Security Requirements based on Azure Security Center assessments (if enabled).</item><item>Automatically updates Threat statuses based on Security Requirement statuses.</item><item>Sends notifications and updates scheduler history.</item></list>
Returns a response model indicating the success or failure of the operation, along with error details if applicable.

requestBody.description: A list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.VPCProjectMappingModel objects representing VNets or Resource Groups to create Threat Models from.

get.summary: Sets the auto-update flag for a Threat Model in the next synchronization cycle.
get.description:
This API updates the scheduler history to enable or disable automatic Threat Model updates during the next synchronization.
- Requires CloudModelerEditionFeature license.
- Returns operation status and error details if applicable.

parameters.name: awsSchedulerHistoryId
parameters.description: The ID of the AWS scheduler history record to update.

parameters.name: autoUpdateThreatModelInNextSync
parameters.description: Indicates whether the Threat Model should be auto-updated in the next sync. Default is false.

post.summary: Synchronizes and saves Azure Virtual Networks (VNets) and Resource Groups for the specified third party information.
post.description:
This API connects to Azure using the provided credentials, retrieves all VNets and Resource Groups, and saves them to the database.
- If isSyncMode is `false`, the third party info is created or updated.
- VNets and Resource Groups are mapped and stored for further threat modeling.
- Logs are written to the AzurePortalIntegration.txt file for diagnostic purposes.

parameters.name: ThirdPartyInfoId
parameters.description: The ID of the third party information used to retrieve credentials if not provided in the model.

parameters.name: isSyncMode
parameters.description: Indicates whether the operation is in sync mode. If true, third party info is not saved; otherwise, it is created or updated.

requestBody.description: The third party information model containing Azure credentials and metadata.

post.summary: Receives Azure Board webhook events and updates Jira status accordingly.

requestBody.description: The Azure Board webhook event payload.

get.summary: Synchronizes SCF (Secure Control Framework) details from the repository to GitHub.
get.description:
This endpoint retrieves SCF domains, controls, compliance frameworks, and compliance framework sections,
serializes them, and creates or updates corresponding files in the configured GitHub repository.

400.description: Feature is not supported or an error occurred during synchronization.

get.summary: Retrieves and synchronizes resource type value details from the repository to GitHub.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches all resource type values and their relationships, serializes them,
and creates or updates corresponding files in the configured GitHub repository.

200.description: Resource type value details were synchronized successfully.

400.description: Feature is not supported or an error occurred during synchronization.

get.summary: Downloads content details for a specified entity from the configured GitHub repository.
get.description:
This endpoint iterates through all configured language models, retrieves the content for the specified entity from GitHub,
and returns the result in the response model.

parameters.name: entityName
parameters.description: The name of the entity whose content details are to be downloaded.

400.description: Feature is not supported or an error occurred during download.

get.summary: Compares the content between the base branch and the head branch in the configured GitHub repository.
get.description:
This endpoint iterates through all configured language models and compares the content between the base branch and the head branch.
The comparison may include pre-release content and local version information as configured.

400.description: Feature is not supported or an error occurred during comparison.

post.summary: Applies a set of diagram transactions (changes) to a Threat Model diagram.
post.description:
Use this API to apply a batch of changes (add, update, delete) to a Threat Model diagram.
- The project must be active and not completed.
- The user must have permission to update the diagram.
- Returns a list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.DiagramChangesResponseModel objects representing the results of each change.
- If threats are affected, the threat risk is updated for the project.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model project.

requestBody.description: A list of diagram change records to apply.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Threat Model is archived or access is denied.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

Use this call to retrieve a list of all the components within the ThreatModeler platform.

get.summary: Gets the secondary property (such as risk color adornments) for all components in a Threat Model diagram.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve secondary properties (e.g., risk color adornments) for all components in the specified Threat Model diagram.
- The Threat Model must be active.
- Returns a ThreatModeler.UI.Models.DiagramElementWithRisk object containing secondary property details.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model.

200.description: OK. Returns secondary property information for diagram components.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Threat Model is archived or access is denied.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary: Gets the secondary property (such as risk color adornments) for a specific diagram element.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve secondary properties (e.g., risk color adornments) for a specific diagram element in the Threat Model diagram.
- Returns a ThreatModeler.UI.Models.DiagramElementIdWithColor object containing secondary property details.

parameters.description: The ID of the diagram element.

post.summary: Gets the secondary property (such as risk color adornments) for a list of diagram elements.

- Returns a list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.DiagramElementIdWithColor objects containing secondary property details for each specified diagram element.

requestBody.description: A list of diagram element IDs for which to retrieve secondary properties.

200.description: OK. Returns secondary property information for the specified diagram elements.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary: Suggests a diagram element (link or group) for dropping a security control in a Threat Model diagram.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a suggestion for where to drop a security control in the diagram.
- Returns a list containing a ThreatModeler.UI.Models.SecurityControlSuggestionModel object with the suggested element ID and type ("Link" or "Group").
- If links exist in the diagram, a random link is suggested.
- If no links exist, a random group (Container, TrustBoundary, or Collection) is suggested.

parameters.name: projectGuid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model project.

parameters.name: securityControlComponentId
parameters.description: The ID of the security control component.

200.description: OK. Returns a suggestion for dropping the security control.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Threat Model is archived or access is denied.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

post.summary: Determines which threats can be mitigated by a specified security control for a given node or link in the diagram.
post.description:
Use this API to retrieve threats that are mitigatable by a security control for a specific node or link.
- If the type is Node, outgoing nodes are analyzed.
- If the type is Link, outgoing nodes from the link are analyzed.
- Only threats associated with the specified security control are returned.
- Throws System.ArgumentException if the security control is invalid.

requestBody.description: The request model containing the diagram object type (Node or Link), the object ID, and the Security Control ID.

get.summary: Exports the diagram for a particular Threat Model in the ThreatModeler Platform.
get.description:
### REMARKS ###
Use this API to export the diagram for a specific Threat Model within the ThreatModeler platform.
- The user must have access to the project.
- The project must be active.
- The license must allow assist functionality.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model to export.

200.description: OK. Returns the exported diagram data.

post.summary: Imports a diagram for a particular Threat Model in the ThreatModeler Platform.
post.description:

- The user must have access to the project.
- The project must be active.
- The license must allow assist functionality.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model to import the diagram into.

requestBody.description: The Graph Links Model representing the diagram to import.

200.description: OK. Returns the result of the import operation.

post.summary: Imports template diagrams for a particular Threat Model in the ThreatModeler Platform.
post.description:
### REMARKS ###
Use this API to import one or more template diagrams for a specific Threat Model within the ThreatModeler platform.
- The user must have access to the project.
- The project must be active.
- The license must allow assist functionality.
- Each template diagram model should represent a valid template to be imported.
- Returns a response indicating the success or failure of the import operation.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model to import templates into.

requestBody.description: A list of template diagram models to import.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Threat Model is archived or access is denied.

400.description: Bad Request. Unauthorized access or error occurred while importing templates.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of threat models for the toolbox, excluding the current project.
get.description:
Use this API to get a list of other threat models (projects) available for toolbox usage, excluding the current project.
- The user must have access to the project.
- The project must be active.
- Returns a ThreatModeler.UI.Models.GraphLinksModel containing node data for each available project.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the current Threat Model project.

200.description: OK. Returns a list of threat models for the toolbox.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Threat Model is archived or access is denied.

400.description: Bad Request. Unauthorized access or error occurred while retrieving threat models.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary:
Gets a summary of the impact of security controls for a specified Threat Model diagram.
Retrieves a summary of the impact of security controls for a specified Threat Model diagram.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a summary of which threats or security requirements can be mitigated by security controls in the specified Threat Model diagram.
- If the default mitigation type is Security Requirement, returns implementable security controls for security requirements.
- Otherwise, returns mitigatable security controls for threats.
- The Threat Model must be active.
- Returns a summary of security control impact for the Threat Model diagram.

parameters.name: projectGuid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model diagram for which to retrieve the security control impact summary.

200.description: OK. Returns a summary of security control impact for the Threat Model diagram.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary: Gets a summary of the impact of security controls for a specified Threat Model diagram.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a summary of which threats or security requirements can be mitigated by security controls in the specified Threat Model diagram.
- If the default mitigation type is Security Requirement, returns implementable security controls for security requirements.
- Otherwise, returns mitigatable security controls for threats.
- The Threat Model must be active.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model diagram.

200.description: OK. Returns a summary of security control impact for the Threat Model diagram.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.summary: Gets a summary of the impact of a specific security control for a Threat Model diagram.
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a summary of which threats or security requirements can be mitigated by the specified security control in the Threat Model diagram.
- If the default mitigation type is Security Requirement, returns implementable security controls for security requirements.
- Otherwise, returns mitigatable security controls for threats.
- The Threat Model must be active.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model diagram.

parameters.name: securityControlDiagramElementId
parameters.description: The diagram element ID of the security control.

200.description: OK. Returns a summary of security control impact for the Threat Model diagram.

post.summary: Applies a security control to a Threat Model diagram element, mitigating threats or implementing security requirements.
post.description:
Use this API to apply a security control to a diagram element in a Threat Model.
- If mitigationType is "SecurityRequirement", implements security requirements.
- Otherwise, mitigates threats.
- Updates third-party status if mitigation is successful.
- If CVE IDs are provided, mitigates CVE threats and updates third-party status.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model diagram.

parameters.name: maxDepth
parameters.description: Optional. The maximum depth for mitigation propagation. If null, uses the default or rule engine value.

parameters.name: mitigationType
parameters.description: Optional. The mitigation type ("Threat" or "SecurityRequirement"). If null, uses the default from configuration.

requestBody.description: The request model containing the Security Control diagram element ID, threat IDs to mitigate, and CVE IDs.

200.description: OK. Security control applied successfully or with warnings.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

post.summary: Applies a security control in the diagram path for a specified Threat Model.
post.description:
Use this API to apply a security control to a diagram element in a Threat Model, considering rule engine configuration.
- If a rule is defined for the security control, the rule engine event is triggered.
- Otherwise, the standard mitigation logic is applied.
- Updates third-party status if mitigation is successful.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model diagram.

parameters.name: mitigationType
parameters.description: Optional. The mitigation type ("Threat" or "SecurityRequirement"). If null, uses the default from configuration.

requestBody.description: The request model containing the Security Control diagram element ID, threat IDs to mitigate, and CVE IDs.

200.description: OK. Security control applied successfully or with warnings.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

<list type="number"><item>If the Threat Model is already created from the VPC, a new record is saved in the VPCProjectMapping table, then fetched from AWS.</item><item>Calls the GCP Accelerator API to create the Threat Model using the provided network information.</item><item>Sends notifications to the user regarding the creation process.</item><item>If the Threat Model is created successfully, associates the project ID with the VPC mapping and updates the scheduler sync details.</item><item>If an error occurs, sends a failure notification and deletes the project if necessary.</item></list>

parameters.description: The name to assign to the third-party info. If null or empty, uses the existing name.

parameters.name: isNameChange
parameters.description: Indicates whether the third-party info name should be updated for all associated projects.

a new Threat Model is created in the platform using the issue details and optional project metadata from the description.
- The API key must be provided in the query string as "X-ThreatModeler-ApiKey".
- The user associated with the API key must exist.
- If a template is specified, the diagram is imported from the template.
- Group access and integration settings are applied if provided.
- Notification emails are sent to the reporter and/or integration user.
- Project status is updated to "Created" after successful creation.

post.summary:
Processes NVD (National Vulnerability Database) files by fetching CVE data from the NVD API,
saving the data as JSON files, zipping the files, extracting them, and importing the CVE data.
post.description:
This endpoint performs the following steps:
1. Fetches CVE data from the NVD API (version 2.0) using pagination.
2. Saves each page of results as a JSON file in a dated directory.
3. Zips all JSON files into a single archive.
4. Extracts the zip archive and processes each JSON file:
   - Validates the file format.
   - Parses and imports the CVE data using the NVD parser.
   - Deletes the extracted directory after successful import.
5. Returns a response indicating the success or failure of the operation.

200.description: Returns the result of the NVD file processing.

400.description: If the request is invalid or file format is incorrect.

500.description: If an internal error occurs during processing.

post.summary: Submits a workflow approval request for a Threat Model from the Diagram screen.
post.description:
When a workflow step is created, a notification is sent for approval or denial.
This endpoint:
<list type="bullet"><item>Requires the CanSubmitAThreatModelForApproval feature authorization.</item><item>Checks if workflow is allowed for the current license.</item><item>Validates that the project is active and not completed.</item><item>Prevents duplicate approval requests for the same approval level.</item><item>On success, sends email notifications to approvers and collaborators.</item></list>

requestBody.description: The ThreatModeler.UI.Models.ProjectWorkflowModel containing workflow details for the approval request.

200.description: Returns the result of the approval request operation.

400.description: Returned when the feature is not supported, the project is archived or completed, or a duplicate approval request exists.

post.summary: Accept a Workflow request from the Threat Model Diagram screen
post.description: When workflow step is created, notification is sent for approval or deny.

post.summary: Denies a workflow approval request for a Threat Model from the Diagram screen.
post.description:
When a workflow step is created, a notification is sent for approval or denial.
This endpoint denies a pending workflow approval request for the specified Threat Model.
<list type="bullet"><item>Requires the CanApproveOrRejectAThreatModel feature authorization.</item><item>Checks if workflow is allowed for the current license.</item><item>Validates that the project is active and not completed.</item><item>Prevents the submitter from rejecting their own request.</item><item>On success, sends email notifications to collaborators.</item></list>

requestBody.description: The ThreatModeler.UI.Models.ProjectWorkflowModel containing workflow details for the rejection request.

200.description: Returns the result of the rejection operation.

400.description: Returned when the feature is not supported, the project is archived or completed, or no pending request exists.

post.summary: Creates a note for a Project Threat, Project Security Requirement, or Project Test Case.
post.description:
Use this API to add a note to a Project Threat, Project Security Requirement, or Project Test Case.
- If the note is empty, the operation will return a success message indicating the note parameter is null.
- If the note is associated with a third-party integration, the note will be added via the integration tool or ALM tool.
- The note is also stored in the ThreatModeler platform for email notification purposes.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of note being created. Default is 0.

requestBody.description: The ThreatModeler.UI.Models.ProjectNoteRequestModel containing the note details, including source information and note text.

200.description: Returns the result of the note creation operation.

400.description: Returned when an error occurs during note creation.

Use this API to add notes to Project Test Cases. If a note is empty, the operation will return a success message indicating the note parameter is null.
If the test case is associated with a third-party integration, the note will be added via the integration tool or ALM tool.
The note is also stored in the ThreatModeler platform for email notification purposes.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of note being created. Default is 0.

requestBody.description: A list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.ProjectNoteRequestModel objects containing the note details for each test case.

200.description: Returns the result of the note creation operation.

400.description: Returned when an error occurs during note creation.

post.summary: Creates notes for Project Threats, Project Security Requirements, or Project Test Cases.
post.description:
Use this API to add notes to Project Threats, Project Security Requirements, or Project Test Cases.
- If the note is empty, the operation will return a success message indicating the note parameter is null.
- If the note is associated with a third-party integration, the note will be added via the integration tool or ALM tool.
- The note is also stored in the ThreatModeler platform for email notification purposes.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of note being created. Default is 0.

parameters.name: isCustomSRUpdate
parameters.description: Indicates whether the note creation is part of a custom Security Requirement update. Default is false.

requestBody.description: A list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.ProjectNoteRequestModel objects containing the note details to be created.

200.description: Returns the result of the note creation operation.

400.description: Returned when an error occurs during note creation.

Retrieves the list of rule engine workflows.
get.description:
This API endpoint reads the "RuleEngine/Rules.json" file and returns its contents as a list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.RuleEngine.RulesEngineWorkflow objects.
If the file exists, it migrates the rules to the database and renames the file to indicate deprecation.
If the file does not exist, it fetches the workflows from the database and generates allowed actions for threat models.

200.description: OK. Returns the list of rule engine workflows.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred while fetching rule engine workflows.

get.summary: Retrieves all email templates used by the Rule Engine.
get.description:
This API endpoint reads the "RuleEngine/EmailTemplate.json" file and returns its contents as a list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.RuleEngineEmailTemplateModel objects.
If the file does not exist, it creates an empty template file.
On success, the response contains the list of email templates ordered by last modification date.
On failure, `IsSuccess` is set to `false` and the error message is included.

200.description: OK. Returns the list of email templates.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred while fetching email templates.

get.summary: Retrieves an email template by its GUID from the Rule Engine.
get.description:
This API endpoint reads the "RuleEngine/EmailTemplate.json" file and returns the email template matching the specified GUID.
<list type="bullet"><item>If the template file does not exist, a System.IO.FileNotFoundException is thrown.</item><item>If the template exists, the template with the specified GUID is returned.</item><item>On success, `IsSuccess` is set to `true`.</item><item>On failure, `IsSuccess` is set to `false` and the error message is included.</item></list>

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the email template to retrieve.

200.description: OK. Returns the email template matching the specified GUID.

400.description: Bad Request. The template file does not exist.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred while fetching the email template.

get.summary: Retrieves the allowed actions for a Threat Model project based on its current status.
get.description:
This API endpoint reads the "RuleEngine/ThreatModelActionsByStatus.json" file and returns the allowed actions for the specified Threat Model project.
- If the file exists, the allowed actions are determined by the project's current status.
- If the file does not exist, an empty ThreatModeler.UI.Models.RuleEngine.RulesEngineThreatModelAllowedActions object is returned.
- If the project is not found, an error message is returned.

parameters.name: Guid
parameters.description: The GUID of the Threat Model project for which to retrieve allowed actions.

200.description: OK. Returns the allowed actions for the Threat Model project.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred while fetching allowed actions for the Threat Model project.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of components and their properties supported by the Rule Engine.
get.description:
This API endpoint returns a list of ThreatModeler.UI.Models.RuleEngine.AcceleratorSupportedRuleEngineComponentModel objects representing components and their properties
that are available for use in the Rule Engine. The result is filtered based on the current configuration setting `ShowResourceDetail`.
- If `ShowResourceDetail` is enabled, the list is populated from the repository.
- On success, the response contains the list of supported components and `IsSuccess` is set to `true`.
- On failure, `IsSuccess` is set to `false` and the error message is logged.

200.description: OK. Returns the list of supported components and their properties for the Rule Engine.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred while fetching the component properties for the Rule Engine.

post.description:
This endpoint queries the ML server for component replacement suggestions based on the provided node data.
If ML suggestions are available and ignoreML is `false`, the top suggestions are returned.
Otherwise, all allowed components are returned. The response is serialized using default contract resolver to preserve property casing.

parameters.name: ignoreTrustBoundary
parameters.description: Indicates whether to ignore trust boundary filtering when retrieving components.

parameters.name: ignoreML
parameters.description: Indicates whether to ignore machine learning (ML) server suggestions and use default allowed components. Default is `false`.

requestBody.description: The node data representing the diagram component for which suggestions are requested.
